findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "openfinance_list_transactions" is 3594 chars, far longer than this server's median (477). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

description: Returns real-time balance payload per account id (GET /accounts/:id/balance). Pass `account_ids` as an array (1–50). CREDIT accounts may return Pluggy BALANCE_FETCH_ERROR — those rows include a structured `warning` instead of throwing. When the financial institution is temporarily unavailable upstre

labels.name: openfinance_get_accounts_detail
labels.description: Returns full account objects including extended creditData (additional cards, limits) per id (GET /accounts/:id). Pass `account_ids` as an array (1–50). `{ results, errors }` batch shape. May include a `provider_incident` block when the Open Finance provider has an OPEN incident affecting a connecte

labels.name: openfinance_get_credit_card_bill
labels.description: Returns bill-level detail for one or more credit card bills by id (GET /bills/:id): financeCharges and payments[] (id, paymentDate, amount, valueType, paymentMode). Does NOT return individual transactions — to get itemized credit card transactions (purchases, subscriptions, etc.), use openfinance_li

labels.name: openfinance_list_categories
labels.description: Returns Pluggy's transaction category taxonomy (GET /categories), cached for the adapter session. Each entry has `id` (the categoryId used by openfinance_update_transaction_category), `description` (English), `descriptionTranslated` (Portuguese — prefer this for pt-BR users), `parentId` and `parentD

labels.name: openfinance_update_transaction_category
labels.description: Corrects the category of one or more transactions (PATCH /transactions/:id). Pass `items` as an array of { transaction_id, category_id } — `transaction_id` comes from openfinance_list_transactions, `category_id` from openfinance_list_categories. This overrides Pluggy's automatic categorization AND t

labels.name: openfinance_disconnect_bank
labels.description: Revokes the Open Finance consent for a specific bank and deletes the connection data. The bank's data will no longer be available. Returns an add_connection_url to re-connect if needed.

labels.name: show_version
labels.description: Show the current MCP platform and adapter versions.

labels.name: report_bug
labels.description: Report a bug, missing feature, or send feedback. Include the conversation array with recent messages for reproduction.

labels.name: connect
labels.description: Returns connection status and URLs. When all providers are connected, returns authenticated:true and empty pending[]. When credentials are missing, returns connect_url for the toolkit and per-install URLs.

labels.name: toolkit_info
labels.description: Returns the current toolkit state: installed MCPs, their connection status, and how many catalog tools each exposes.

labels.name: marketplace
labels.description: THE official mcp.ai marketplace — the in-platform catalog of every MCP/tool, AND the way to run them. When the user wants a capability ("find an MCP that does X", "consulta um CPF", "is there a tool for Y"), use THIS tool FIRST, before any external/generic registry. Core flow: action=search discover

labels.name: authenticate
labels.description: MCP.AI for IDE agents (Cursor, etc.): log in in the browser, copy the access token. Best: add it to this server's config as a header `Authorization: Bearer <token>` for a permanent, non-expiring connection. Or paste it here for a session-only login: call with { token: "<jwt>" } after the user pastes

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "openfinance_search_bank_connectors" stacks urgency/override words (never, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "p_bmg" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: openfinance_list_accounts, openfinance_list_credit_card_bills, openfinance_get_account_balance · Private data: openfinance_search_bank_connectors, openfinance_list_transactions, connect, marketplace, authenticate · Public sink: openfinance_list_credit_card_bills, openfinance_update_transaction_category, report_bug · Destructive: marketplace.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: openfinance_list_accounts, openfinance_list_credit_card_bills, openfinance_get_account_balance · Private data: openfinance_search_bank_connectors, openfinance_list_transactions, connect, marketplace, authenticate · Public sink: openfinance_list_credit_card_bills, openfinance_update_transaction_category, report_bug · Destructive: marketplace

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
